text:
Two things are simultaneously true:

1) Nothing compares to the impact of dramatically reducing CO2 emissions immediately. 

2) We should invest in long-shot ideas to reduce CO2, because even when we hit net zero emissions we'll want to reduce the severity of impacts.
author: did:plc:xc5j6qxxp4hpdeyyx5w6rhco

text:
A new EPA report shows that while acceptance of climate change is high in Europe, more needs to be done to communicate the human driven nature of the growing crisis.

This project, led by Dr Ashling Bourke, contains findings on attitudes to climate change.

Read more here: launch.dcu.ie/49hatQ0

text:
"You probably feel bad because you're stressed."
Sure but a normal bodily response is not immediate symptoms like these. Before you respond to someone with a chronic health condition, maybe ask yourself why we're reaching out in the first place. More than likely we need empathy. Not this response.

text: It's, therefore, uncommon to see a good that from beginning to end only involves Americans in America with 100%  American parts. That's why this number is less than some percent of GDP routinely given; those numbers are counting any part made or people in the process located in the United States.
